step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture shows a suggestive search pipeline. First, user actions on the mobile app are ingested into Kinesis Data Streams. Next, Kinesis Data Analytics filters search queries using SQL and publishes them to a second Kinesis stream. A Lambda transformer enriches these queries via the Search API and saves them to DocumentDB. On a schedule, a producer Lambda reads from DocumentDB, checks item availability via the Inventory API, and writes active search suggestions to Redis (ElastiCache). Finally, the Search API reads from Redis to serve recommendations back to the user.

graph.name: Zulily Suggestive Search Architecture
graph.notes: Suggestive search experience driven by real-time clickstream data, processing ~1000 TPS on DocumentDB with a 7-day TTL and primary/replica configuration.

nodes.name: Mobile App User
nodes.notes: Generates clickstream data and queries the Search API.

nodes.name: Clickstream Kinesis Stream
nodes.notes: Ingests raw clickstream events in real time.

nodes.name: Kinesis Data Analytics
nodes.notes: Filters out search keywords using simple SQL queries.

nodes.name: Transformer Lambda
nodes.notes: Enriches keywords with brand/category data and writes to DocumentDB.

nodes.name: DocumentDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: 650M documents, 3 AZs (1 primary, 2 replicas), 7-day TTL.

nodes.name: Producer Lambda
nodes.notes: Runs on a schedule to validate search data against stock inventory.

nodes.name: Inventory API
nodes.notes: Checks current product stock levels (products rotate every 72 hours).

nodes.name: Redis
nodes.notes: Caches validated search suggestions for low-latency retrieval.

nodes.name: Search API
nodes.notes: Serves the suggestions to the customer's mobile app.

edges.notes: Lambda pulls search keywords on schedule.
Delivers suggestive search results back to user.
